1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: WAS THERE NOT a time in the life of man when he was not even a mentionable thing?

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Indeed there has been a time for man, when even his name did not exist anywhere.

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Has there come on man a while of time when he was a thing unremembered?

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Surely there hath come upon man a space of time when he was not a thing worth mentioning.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Has there not been over man a period of time, when he was nothing to be mentioned?

1.author: Itani
1.text: Has there come upon man a period of time when he was nothing to be mentioned?

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Was there a period of time when man was not even worthy of a mention?

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Has there not been over man a period of time, when he was not a thing worth mentioning

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Hath there come upon man (ever) any period of time in which he was a thing unremembered?

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Has there been a period of time for man when he was not anything worthy of mention?

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Indeed, there came upon the human a period of time when he was an unremembered thing.

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Has there [not] come upon man a period of time when he was not a thing [even] mentioned?

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: There was certainly a time when there was no mention of the human being.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: There surely came over man a period of time when he was a thing not worth mentioning.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Was there not a period of time when man was nothing worth mentioning?

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Has there not been over Man a long period of Time, when he was nothing - (not even) mentioned?

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Hal ata AAala alinsani heenun mina alddahri lam yakun shayan mathkooran

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Verily We created man from a sperm yoked (to the ovum) to bring out his real substance, then gave him hearing and sight.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Indeed We have created man from mixed semen; in order to test him – We therefore made him hearing, knowing.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: We created man of a sperm-drop, a mingling, and We made him hearing, seeing.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Verily We! We created man from a sperm of mixtures, that We might prove him, wherefore We made him hearing, seeing.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Verily, We have created man from Nutfah drops of mixed semen (discharge of man and woman), in order to try him, so We made him hearer, seer.

2.author: Itani
2.text: We created man from a liquid mixture, to test him; and We made him hearing and seeing.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Verily We created man out of a drop of intermingled sperm so that We might try him, and We therefore endowed him with hearing and sight.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Verily, We have created man from Nutfah Amshaj, in order to try him, so, We made him hearer and seer.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Lo! We create man from a drop of thickened fluid to test him; so We make him hearing, knowing.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Indeed We created man from the drop of a mixed fluid so that We may put him to test, so We endowed with hearing and sight.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: We have created the human from a (sperm) drop, a mixture, testing him; We made him to hear and see.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Indeed, We created man from a sperm-drop mixture that We may try him; and We made him hearing and seeing.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: We created the human being from the union of sperm and egg to test him. We gave him hearing and vision.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Surely We have created man from a small life-germ uniting (itself): We mean to try him, so We have made him hearing, seeing.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: We created man from a drop of mingled fluid so that We might try him; We gave him hearing and sight;

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Verily We created Man from a drop of mingled sperm, in order to try him: So We gave him (the gifts), of Hearing and Sight.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Inna khalaqna alinsana min nutfatin amshajin nabtaleehi fajaAAalnahu sameeAAan baseeran

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: We surely showed him the way that he may either be grateful or deny.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: We have indeed shown him the way – whether he is grateful or ingrate.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Surely We guided him upon the way whether he be thankful or unthankful.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Verily We! We shewed him the way; then he becometh either thankful or ingrate.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Verily, We showed him the way, whether he be grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Itani
3.text: We guided him to the way, be he appreciative or unappreciative.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Surely We showed him the Right Path, regardless of whether he chooses to be thankful or unthankful (to his Lord).

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Verily, We guided him to the way, whether he be grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Lo! We have shown him the way, whether he be grateful or disbelieving.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Indeed We have guided him to the way, be he grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Indeed, We have guided him to the path, he is either grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Indeed, We guided him to the way, be he grateful or be he ungrateful.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: We showed him the right path whether he would be grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Surely We have shown him the way: he may be thankful or unthankful.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: We showed him the way, whether he be grateful or ungrateful.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: We showed him the Way: whether he be grateful or ungrateful (rests on his will).

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Inna hadaynahu alssabeela imma shakiran waimma kafooran

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: We have prepared for unbelievers chains and collars and a blazing fire.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: We have indeed kept prepared chains, and shackles and a blazing fire for the disbelievers.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Surely We have prepared for the unbelievers chains, fetters, and a Blaze.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Verily We! We have gotten ready for the infidels chains and collars and a Blaze.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Verily, We have prepared for the disbelievers iron chains, iron collars, and a blazing Fire.

4.author: Itani
4.text: We have prepared for the faithless chains, and yokes, and a Searing Fire.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: For the unbelievers, We have kept ready chains and fetters and a Blazing Fire.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Verily, We have prepared for the disbelievers iron chains, iron collars, and Sa`ir.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Lo! We have prepared for disbelievers manacles and carcans and a raging fire.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: Indeed for the faithless We have prepared chains, iron collars, and a blaze.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: Indeed, for the unbelievers We have prepared chains, fetters and a Blazing (Fire).

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains and shackles and a blaze.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: We have prepared chains, shackles, and flaming fire (for the disbelievers).

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Surely We have prepared for the unbelievers chains and shackles and a burning fire.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: [Now,] behold, for those who deny the truth, We have prepared chains, iron collars and a blazing fire, but

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: For the Rejecters we have prepared chains, yokes, and a blazing Fire.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Inna aAAtadna lilkafireena salasila waaghlalan wasaAAeeran

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Surely the devotees will drink cups flavoured with palm blossoms

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Indeed the virtuous will drink from a cup, containing a mixture of Kafoor.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Surely the pious shall drink of a cup whose mixture is camphor,

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Verily the pious shall drink of a cup whereof the admixture is like Unto camphor.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Verily, the Abrar (pious, who fear Allah and avoid evil), shall drink a cup (of wine) mixed with water from a spring in Paradise called Kafur.

5.author: Itani
5.text: But the righteous will drink from a cup whose mixture is aroma.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: The virtuous shall drink from a cup tempered with camphor water.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Verily, the Abrar (righteous believers) shall drink of a cup mixed with Kafur.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Lo! the righteous shall drink of a cup whereof the mixture is of Kafur,

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Indeed the pious will drink from a cup seasoned with Kafur,

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: But the righteous shall drink of a goblet mixed with camphor;

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of Kafur,

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: The virtuous ones will drink from a cup containing camphor

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Surely the righteous shall drink of a cup the admixture of which is camphor

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: the righteous shall drink from a cup mixed with the coolness of kafur,

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: As to the Righteous, they shall drink of a Cup (of Wine) mixed with Kafur,-

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Inna alabrara yashraboona min kasin kana mizajuha kafooran

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: From a spring of which the votaries of God will drink and make it flow in abundance.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: The Kafoor is a spring, from which the chosen bondmen of Allah will drink, causing it to flow wherever they wish inside their palaces.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: a fountain whereat drink the servants of God, making it to gush forth plenteously.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: From a fountain whence the bondmen of Allah will drink, causing it to gush abundantly.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: A spring wherefrom the slaves of Allah will drink, causing it to gush forth abundantly.

6.author: Itani
6.text: A spring from which the servants of God will drink, making it gush abundantly.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: This will be a gushing spring wherefrom Allah's servants shall drink wine, a spring from which they will take out channels wherever they wish.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: A spring wherefrom the servants of Allah will drink, causing it to gush forth abundantly.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: A spring wherefrom the slaves of Allah drink, making it gush forth abundantly,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: a spring where Allah’s servants will drink, making it gush forth as they please.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: a fountain at which the worshipers of Allah drink and make it gush forth abundantly;

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: A spring of which the [righteous] servants of Allah will drink; they will make it gush forth in force [and abundance].

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: which flows from a spring from which the servants of God will drink.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: A fountain from which the servants of Allah shall drink; they make it to flow a (goodly) flowing forth.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: a spring from which God's servants will drink, making it gush forth in branches.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: A Fountain where the Devotees of Allah do drink, making it flow in unstinted abundance.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: AAaynan yashrabu biha AAibadu Allahi yufajjiroonaha tafjeeran

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Those who fulfil their vows and fear the Day whose evil shall be diffused far and wide,

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: They fulfil their pledges, and fear a day the evil of which is widespread.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: They fulfil their vows, and fear a day whose evil is upon the wing;

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: They fulfill their vow and dread a Day the evil whereof shall be widespreading.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: They (are those who) fulfill (their) vows, and they fear a Day whose evil will be wide-spreading.

7.author: Itani
7.text: They fulfill their vows, and dread a Day whose ill is widespread.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: These will be the ones who fulfil their vows and dread the Day whose woe shall be spread far and wide;

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: They fulfill (their) vows, and they fear a Day whose evil will be wide-spreading.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: (Because) they perform the vow and fear a day whereof the evil is wide-spreading,

7.author: Qarai
7.text: They fulfill their vows and fear a day whose ill will be widespread.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: they who keep their vows and fear a Day whose evil is spread;

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: They [are those who] fulfill [their] vows and fear a Day whose evil will be widespread.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: The servants of God fulfill their vows and are afraid of the day in which there will be widespread terror.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: They fulfill vows and fear a day the evil of which shall be spreading far and wide.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: They keep their vows and fear a day the woe of which will spread far and wide;

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: They perform (their) vows, and they fear a Day whose evil flies far and wide.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Yoofoona bialnnathri wayakhafoona yawman kana sharruhu mustateeran

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: And feed the needy for the love of Him, and the orphans and the captives,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And out of His love, they give food to the needy, the orphan and the prisoner.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: they give food, for the love of Him, to the needy, the orphan, the captive:

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And they feed with food, for love of Him, the needy, and the orphan and the captive.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And they give food, inspite of their love for it (or for the love of Him), to Miskin (poor), the orphan, and the captive,

8.author: Itani
8.text: And they feed, for the love of Him, the poor, and the orphan, and the captive.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: those who, for the love of Him, feed the needy, and the orphan, and the captive,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And they give food, inspite of their love for it, to the poor, the orphan and the captive,

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And feed with food the needy wretch, the orphan and the prisoner, for love of Him,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: For the love of Him, they feed the needy, the orphan and the prisoner,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: who give food, for the love of Him to the needy, the orphan, and the captive,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive,

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: They feed the destitute, orphans, and captives for the love of God, saying,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And they give food out of love for Him to the poor and the orphan and the captive:

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: they give food, despite their love for it, to the poor and orphans and captives,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And they feed, for the love of Allah, the indigent, the orphan, and the captive,-

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: WayutAAimona alttaAAama AAala hubbihi miskeenan wayateeman waaseeran

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: (Saying): "We feed you for the sake of God, desiring neither recompense nor thanks.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: They say to them, “We give you food, only for the sake of Allah – we do not seek any reward or thanks from you.”

9.author: Arberry
9.text: 'We feed you only for the Face of God; we desire no recompense from you, no thankfulness;

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Saying: we feed you only for the sake of Allah; we desire not from you any recompense or thanks.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: (Saying): "We feed you seeking Allah's Countenance only. We wish for no reward, nor thanks from you.

9.author: Itani
9.text: “We only feed you for the sake of God. We want from you neither compensation, nor gratitude.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: (saying): “We feed you only for Allah's sake; we do not seek of you any recompense or thanks,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: (Saying): "We feed you seeking Allah's Face only. We wish for no reward, nor thanks from you."

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: (Saying): We feed you, for the sake of Allah only. We wish for no reward nor thanks from you;

9.author: Qarai
9.text: [saying,] ‘We feed you only for the sake of Allah. We desire no reward from you, nor thanks.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: (saying): 'We feed you only desirous of the Face of Allah; we seek of you neither recompense nor thanks,

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: [Saying], "We feed you only for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: "We only feed you for the sake of God and we do not want any reward or thanks from you.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: We only feed you for Allah's sake; we desire from you neither reward nor thanks:

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: saying, "We feed you for the sake of God alone, we seek neither recompense nor thanks from you.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: (Saying),"We feed you for the sake of Allah alone: no reward do we desire from you, nor thanks.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Innama nutAAimukum liwajhi Allahi la nureedu minkum jazaan wala shukooran

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: We fear the dismal day calamitous from our Lord."

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: “Indeed we fear from our Lord a day which is extremely bitter, most severe.”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: for we fear from our Lord a frowning day, inauspicious.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Verily we dread from our Lord a Day grim and distressful.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: "Verily, We fear from our Lord a Day, hard and distressful, that will make the faces look horrible (from extreme dislikeness to it)."

10.author: Itani
10.text: We dread from our Lord a frowning grim Day.”

10.author: Maududi
10.text: we fear from our Lord a Day that shall be long and distressful.”

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: "Verily, We fear from our Lord a Day that is `Abus and Qamtarir."

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Lo! we fear from our Lord a day of frowning and of fate.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Indeed we fear a frowning and fateful day from our Lord.’

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: for we fear from our Lord a stern, frownful Day'

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Indeed, We fear from our Lord a Day austere and distressful."

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: We are afraid of our Lord and the bitterly distressful day".

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Surely we fear from our Lord a stern, distressful day.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Truly, we fear from our Lord a woefully grim Day."

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: "We only fear a Day of distressful Wrath from the side of our Lord."

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Inna nakhafu min rabbina yawman AAaboosan qamtareeran

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: So God will protect them from the evil of that day, and grant them happiness and joy,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: So Allah saved them from the evil of that day, and gave them freshness and joy.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: So God has guarded them from the evil of that day, and has procured them radiancy and gladness,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Wherefore Allah shall preserve them from the evil of that Day, and shall cause them to meet brightness and joy.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: So Allah saved them from the evil of that Day, and gave them Nadratan (a light of beauty) and joy.

11.author: Itani
11.text: So God will protect them from the ills of that Day, and will grant them radiance and joy.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: So Allah shall guard them against the woe of that Day, and will procure them freshness and joy,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: So, Allah saved them from the evil of that Day, and gave them Nadrah (a light of beauty) and joy.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Therefor Allah hath warded off from them the evil of that day, and hath made them find brightness and joy;

11.author: Qarai
11.text: So Allah saved them from that day’s ills and graced them with freshness [on this faces] and joy [in their hearts].

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: So Allah will save them from the evil of that Day and has secured for them radiance and joyfulness,

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: So Allah will protect them from the evil of that Day and give them radiance and happiness

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: God will certainly rescue them from the terror of that day and will meet them with joy and pleasure.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Therefore Allah win guard them from the evil of that day and cause them to meet with ease and happiness;

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Therefore, God will ward off from them the woes of that Day, and make them find brightness and joy,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: But Allah will deliver them from the evil of that Day, and will shed over them a Light of Beauty and (blissful) Joy.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Fawaqahumu Allahu sharra thalika alyawmi walaqqahum nadratan wasurooran

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: And reward them for their perseverence Paradise and silken robes,

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And gave them Paradise and silk clothes, as a reward for their patience.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and recompensed them for their patience with a Garden, and silk;

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And shall recompense them for that which they patiently bare with a Garden and silken garment.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And their recompense shall be Paradise, and silken garments, because they were patient.

12.author: Itani
12.text: And will reward them for their patience with a Garden and silk.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and will reward them for their steadfastness with Paradise and robes of silk.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And their recompense shall be Paradise, and silken garments, because they were patient.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And hath awarded them for all that they endured, a Garden and silk attire;

12.author: Qarai
12.text: He rewarded them for their patience with a garden and [garments of] silk,

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: and recompense them for their patience with a Garden, and robes of silk.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And will reward them for what they patiently endured [with] a garden [in Paradise] and silk [garments].

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: For their patience, He will reward them with Paradise and silk.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And reward them, because they were patient, with garden and silk,

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: and their reward for being patient will be a Garden and silk [clothing].

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And because they were patient and constant, He will reward them with a Garden and (garments of) silk.

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: Where they will recline on couches feeling neither heat of the sun nor intense cold.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: Reclining in it, upon thrones; they will not see the hot sunshine in it, nor the bitter cold.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: therein they shall recline upon couches, therein they shall see neither sun nor bitter cold;

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Reclining therein upon couches, they shall behold therein neither sun nor hurting cold.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Reclining therein on raised thrones, they will see there neither the excessive heat of the sun, nor the excessive bitter cold, (as in Paradise there is no sun and no moon).

13.author: Itani
13.text: Reclining therein on the thrones; experiencing therein neither sun, nor frost.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: There they will recline on elevated couches and will be subjected neither to the burning heat of the sun nor to bitter cold.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Reclining therein on raised couches, they will see there neither the excessive heat, nor the excessive cold.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Reclining therein upon couches, they will find there neither (heat of) a sun nor bitter cold.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: reclining therein on couches, without facing any [scorching] sun, or [biting] cold.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: Reclining there upon couches, they shall see neither the sun nor the moon.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: [They will be] reclining therein on adorned couches. They will not see therein any [burning] sun or [freezing] cold.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: They will recline therein on couches and they will find neither excessive heat nor cold.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Reclining therein on raised couches, they shall find therein neither (the severe heat of) the sun nor intense cold.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: Reclining upon couches, they will find therein neither the heat of the sun nor bitter, biting cold,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Reclining in the (Garden) on raised thrones, they will see there neither the sun's (excessive heat) nor (the moon's) excessive cold.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Muttakieena feeha AAala alaraiki la yarawna feeha shamsan wala zamhareeran

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: The shadows will bend over them, and low will hang the clusters of grapes.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And its shade will cover them, and its fruit clusters brought down low for them.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: near them shall be its shades, and its clusters hung meekly down,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And close upon them will be the shades thereof, and low will hang the clusters thereof greatly.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And the shade thereof is close upon them, and the bunches of fruit thereof will hang low within their reach.

14.author: Itani
14.text: Its shade hovering over them, and its fruit brought low within reach.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: The shades of Paradise will bend over them, and its fruits will be brought within their easy reach;

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And the shade thereof is close upon them, and the bunches of fruit thereof will hang low within their reach.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: The shade thereof is close upon them and the clustered fruits thereof bow down.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Its shades will be close over them and its clusters [of fruits] will be hanging low.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Near them shall be its shades with its clusters hung gently down,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And near above them are its shades, and its [fruit] to be picked will be lowered in compliance.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: The shades of the garden will be closely spread over them and it will be easy for them to reach the fruits.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And close down upon them (shall be) its shadows, and its fruits shall be made near (to them), being easy to reach.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: the shading branches of trees will come down low over them, and their clusters of fruit, will hang down where they are the easiest to reach.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And the shades of the (Garden) will come low over them, and the bunches (of fruit), there, will hang low in humility.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Wadaniyatan AAalayhim thilaluha wathullilat qutoofuha tathleelan

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Passed round will be silver flagons and goblets made of glass,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Rounds of silver cups and silver beakers, looking like glass, will be presented upon them.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: and there shall be passed around them vessels of silver, and goblets of crystal,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And brought round amongst them will be vessels of silver and also goblets of glass.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And amongst them will be passed round vessels of silver and cups of crystal,

15.author: Itani
15.text: Passing around them are vessels of silver, and cups of crystal.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: and there shall be passed around them vessels of silver and goblets of crystal,

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And among them will be passed round vessels of silver and cups of crystal --

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Goblets of silver are brought round for them, and beakers (as) of glass

15.author: Qarai
15.text: They will be served around with vessels of silver and goblets of crystal

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: and passed around them shall be silver vessels, and crystal goblets,

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And there will be circulated among them vessels of silver and cups having been [created] clear [as glass],

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: They will be served with silver dishes and crystal clear goblets.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And there shall be made to go round about them vessels of silver and goblets which are of glass,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Vessels of silver and goblets of pure crystal will be passed round among them

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: And amongst them will be passed round vessels of silver and goblets of crystal,-

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Wayutafu AAalayhim bianiyatin min fiddatin waakwabin kanat qawareera

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: And crystal clear bottles of silver, of which they will determine the measure themselves.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: Glass made from silver, which the servers have filled up to the measure.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: crystal of silver that they have measured -- very exactly.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Godblets of silver, they shall have filled them to exact measure.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Crystal-clear, made of silver. They will determine the measure thereof according to their wishes.

16.author: Itani
16.text: Crystal of silver—they measured them exactly.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: goblets bright as crystal but made of silver, filled to exact measure.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: (Qawarir) Crystal-clear, made of silver. They will determine the measure thereof.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: (Bright as) glass but (made) of silver, which they (themselves) have measured to the measure (of their deeds).

16.author: Qarai
16.text: —crystal of silver— [from] which they will dispense in a precise measure.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: goblets of silver that they have precisely measured.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Clear glasses [made] from silver of which they have determined the measure.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Also there will be crystal clear goblets of silver containing the exact measure of drink which they desire.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: (Transparent as) glass, made of silver; they have measured them according to a measure.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: and gleaming silver goblets which have been filled to the exact measure,

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Crystal-clear, made of silver: they will determine the measure thereof (according to their wishes).

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: There will they drink a cup flavoured with ginger

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And in Paradise they will be given to drink cups, filled with a mixture of ginger.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: And therein they shall be given to drink a cup whose mixture is ginger,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And therein they shall be given to drink of a cup whereof the admixture will be ginger.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And they will be given to drink there a cup (of wine) mixed with Zanjabil (ginger, etc.),

17.author: Itani
17.text: They will be served therein with a cup whose flavor is Zanjabeel.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Therein they shall be served a cup flavoured with ginger,

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And they will be given to drink there of a cup (of wine) mixed with Zanjabil (ginger),

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: There are they watered with a cup whereof the mixture is of Zanjabil,

17.author: Qarai
17.text: They will be served therein with a cup of a drink seasoned with Zanjabeel,

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: And they shall be given to drink from a cup whose mixture is ginger,

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And they will be given to drink a cup [of wine] whose mixture is of ginger

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: They will drink cups containing (soft flowing) sparkling water

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And they shall be made to drink therein a cup the admixture of which shall be ginger,

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and they will be given a cup to drink flavoured with ginger,

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And they will be given to drink there of a Cup (of Wine) mixed with Zanjabil,-

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Wayusqawna feeha kasan kana mizajuha zanjabeelan

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: From a spring by the name of Ever-flowing-Salsabil.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Which is a spring in Paradise called Salsabeel.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: therein a fountain whose name is called Salsabil.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: From a fountain therein, named Salsabil.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: drawn from a spring (in Paradise) called Salsabil.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: (The water of) a spring therein, named Salsabil.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: from a spring in it named Salsabeel.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: [From] a fountain within Paradise named Salsabeel.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: (Of) a fountain therein which is named Salsabil.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: from a flowing spring called Salsabil.

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: And boys of everlasting youth will go about attending them. Looking at them you would think that they were pearls dispersed.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Immortal youths shall surround them, waiting upon them; when you see them, you would think they are scattered pearls.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: Immortal youths shall go about them; when thou seest them, thou supposest them scattered pearls,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And there shall go round Unto them youths ever-young. When thou seest them thou wouldst deem them pearls unstrung.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And round about them will (serve) boys of everlasting youth. If you see them, you would think them scattered pearls.

19.author: Itani
19.text: Passing among them are eternalized youths. If you see them, you would think them sprinkled pearls.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: There boys of everlasting youth shall go about attending them: when you see them, you would think that they are scattered pearls.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And round about them will (serve) boys of everlasting youth. If you see them, you would think them scattered pearls.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: There wait on them immortal youths, whom, when thou seest, thou wouldst take for scattered pearls.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: They will be waited upon by immortal youths, whom, were you to see them, you will suppose them to be scattered pearls.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Immortal youths will go about them, when you see them, you would suppose them to be scattered pearls.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal. When you see them, you would think them [as beautiful as] scattered pearls.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: They will be served by immortal youths who look like scattered pearls.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And round about them shall go youths never altering in age; when you see them you will think them to be scattered pearls.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: They will be attended by youths who will not age -- when you see them you will think them to be like sprinkled pearls --

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And round about them will (serve) youths of perpetual (freshness): If thou seest them, thou wouldst think them scattered Pearls.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Wayatoofu AAalayhim wildanun mukhalladoona itha raaytahum hasibtahum luluan manthooran

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: When you look around, you will see delights and great dominion.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And when you look towards it, you will see serenity and a great kingdom.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: when thou seest them then thou seest bliss and a great kingdom.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And when thou lookest them thou shalt behold delight and a dominion magnificent.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And when you look there (in Paradise), you will see a delight (that cannot be imagined), and a great dominion.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Wherever you look, you see bliss, and a vast kingdom.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Whitherto you look around, you will see an abundance of bliss and the glories of a great kingdom.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And when you look there (in Paradise), you will see a delight (that cannot be imagined), and a great dominion.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: When thou seest, thou wilt see there bliss and high estate.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: As you look on, you will see there bliss and a great kingdom.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: When you see them, you see bliss, and a great Kingdom.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And when you look there [in Paradise], you will see pleasure and great dominion.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: If you were to see it, you would find it to be a great kingdom with great bounty.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And when you see there, you shall see blessings and a great kingdom.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: wherever you look, you will see bliss and a great kingdom:

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And when thou lookest, it is there thou wilt see a Bliss and a Realm Magnificent.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Waitha raayta thamma raayta naAAeeman wamulkan kabeeran

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: On their bodies will be garments of the finest green silk and brocade, and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver; and their Lord will give them a purest draught to drink.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: In it they adorn clothes of fine green silk and gold embroidery; and they are given silver bracelets to wear; and their Lord gave them pure wine to drink.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Upon them shall be green garments of silk and brocade; they are adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord shall give them to drink a pure draught.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Upon them shall be garments of fine green silk and of brocades. And adorned they shall be with bracelets of silver: and their Lord shall give them drink a beverage pure.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Their garments will be of fine green silk, and gold embroidery. They will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a pure drink.

21.author: Itani
21.text: Upon them are garments of green silk, and satin. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. And their Lord will offer them a pure drink.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: They [i.e., the virtuous] shall be attired in garments of fine green silk and rich brocade and will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them a pure wine to drink.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Their garments will be of fine green silk, and Istabraq. They will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Their raiment will be fine green silk and gold embroidery. Bracelets of silver will they wear. Their Lord will slake their thirst with a pure drink.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Upon them will be cloaks of green silk and brocade and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them to drink a pure drink.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Upon them there will be garments of green silk, rich brocade, and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them a pure beverage to drink.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Upon the inhabitants will be green garments of fine silk and brocade. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: They will have fine green silk and brocade, and they will be decked with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will provide them with a drink of pure wine.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Upon them shall be garments of fine green silk and thick silk interwoven with gold, and they shall be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord shall make them drink a pure drink.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: they will wear green garments of fine silk and rich brocade. They will be adorned with silver bracelets. And their Lord will give them a pure drink.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Upon them will be green Garments of fine silk and heavy brocade, and they will be adorned with Bracelets of silver; and their Lord will give to them to drink of a Wine Pure and Holy.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: AAaliyahum thiyabu sundusin khudrun waistabraqun wahulloo asawira min fiddatin wasaqahum rabbuhum sharaban tahooran

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: "This in truth is your recompense, and acceptance of your endeavours."

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: It will be said to them, “This is your reward – indeed your efforts have been appreciated.”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Behold, this is a recompense for you, and your striving is thanked.'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Verily this is for you by way of recompense, and your endeavour hath been accepted.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: (And it will be said to them): "Verily, this is a reward for you, and your endeavour has been accepted."

22.author: Itani
22.text: “This is a reward for you. Your efforts are well appreciated.”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Behold, this is your recompense and your endeavour has been appreciated.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: (And it will be said to them): "Verily, this is a reward for you, and your endeavor has been accepted."

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: (And it will be said unto them): Lo! this is a reward for you. Your endeavour (upon earth) hath found acceptance.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: [They will be told]: ‘This is your reward, and your efforts have been well-appreciated.’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: 'See, this is your recompense, your striving is thanked'

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: [And it will be said], "Indeed, this is for you a reward, and your effort has been appreciated."

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: This will be their reward and their efforts will be appreciated.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: Surely this is a reward for you, and your striving shall be recompensed.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: This is your reward. Your endeavour is fully acknowledged.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: "Verily this is a Reward for you, and your Endeavour is accepted and recognised."

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Inna hatha kana lakum jazaan wakana saAAyukum mashkooran

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: We have revealed the Qur'an to you gradually;

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Indeed We have sent down the Qur’an upon you, in stages.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: Surely We have sent down the Koran on thee, a sending down;

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Verily We! it is We Who have revealed Unto thee the Qur'an, a gradual revelation.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Verily! It is We Who have sent down the Quran to you (O Muhammad SAW) by stages.

23.author: Itani
23.text: It is We who sent down the Quran upon you—a gradual revelation.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: (O Prophet), indeed We have revealed the Qur'an to you in portions.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Verily, it is We Who have sent down the Qur'an to you by stages.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Lo! We, even We, have revealed unto thee the Qur'an, a revelation;

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Indeed We have sent down to you the Quran in a gradual descent.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: We have indeed, sent down to you the Koran, a (clear) sending,

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Indeed, it is We who have sent down to you, [O Muhammad], the Qur'an progressively.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: (Muhammad), We have revealed the Quran to you in gradual steps.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Surely We Ourselves have revealed the Quran to you revealing (it) in portions.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Truly, it is We who have revealed to you the Quran, a gradual revelation.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: It is We Who have sent down the Qur'an to thee by stages.

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Inna nahnu nazzalna AAalayka alqurana tanzeelan

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: So wait for your Lord's command, and do not follow any sinner or disbeliever among them.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Therefore stay patient upon your Lord’s command, and do not listen to any of the sinners or ingrates among them.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: so be thou patient under the judgment of thy Lord, and obey not one of them, sinner or unbeliever.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Wherefore persevere thou with the commandment of thy Lord, and obey not thou of them, any sinner or ingrate.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Therefore be patient (O Muhammad SAW) and submit to the Command of your Lord (Allah, by doing your duty to Him and by conveying His Message to mankind), and obey neither a sinner nor a disbeliever among them.

24.author: Itani
24.text: So be patient for the decision of your Lord, and do not obey the sinner or the blasphemer among them.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: So persevere with the command of your Lord and do not pay any heed to the wicked and the unbelieving,

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Therefore be patient with constancy to the command of your Lord, and obey neither a sinner nor a disbeliever among them.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: So submit patiently to thy Lord's command, and obey not of them any guilty one or disbeliever.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord and do not obey any sinner or ingrate from among them,

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: therefore be patient under the Judgement of your Lord, and do not obey either the sinner or the unbeliever.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: So be patient for the decision of your Lord and do not obey from among them a sinner or ungrateful [disbeliever].

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: So wait patiently for the command of your Lord and do not yield to any sinful or disbelieving person among them (people).

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Therefore wait patiently for the command of your Lord, and obey not from among them a sinner or an ungrateful one.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: So wait patiently for the command of your Lord, and do not yield to anyone among them who is sinful or ungrateful;

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Therefore be patient with constancy to the Command of thy Lord, and hearken not to the sinner or the ingrate among them.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Faisbir lihukmi rabbika wala tutiAA minhum athiman aw kafooran

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Meditate upon the name of your Lord morning and evening;

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And remember the name of your Lord morning and evening.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And remember the Name of thy Lord at dawn and in the evening

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And remember thou the name of thy Lord in the morning and in the evening.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And remember the Name of your Lord every morning and afternoon [i.e. offering of the Morning (Fajr), Zuhr, and 'Asr prayers].

25.author: Itani
25.text: And mention the Name of your Lord, morning and evening.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: and remember the name of your Lord, morning and evening;

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And remember the Name of your Lord every morning and afternoon.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Remember the name of thy Lord at morn and evening.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: and celebrate the Name of your Lord morning and evening,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: And, remember the Name of your Lord at the dawn and in the evening;

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And mention the name of your Lord [in prayer] morning and evening

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Mention the Name of your Lord, mornings and evenings.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And glorify the name of your Lord morning and evening.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: and glorify your Lord morning and evening;

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: And celebrate the name of thy Lord morning and evening,

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: And bow in homage part of the night, and glorify Him far into the night.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And prostrate for Him in a part of the night, and proclaim His purity into the long night.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: and part of the night; bow down before Him and magnify Him through the long night.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And during the night - worship Him; and hallow Him the livelong night.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And during night, prostrate yourself to Him (i.e. the offering of Maghrib and 'Isha' prayers), and glorify Him a long night through (i.e. Tahajjud prayer).

26.author: Itani
26.text: And for part of the night, prostrate yourself to Him, and glorify Him long into the night.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: and prostrate yourself before Him at night, and extol His Glory during the long watches of the night.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And during the night, prostrate yourself to Him, and glorify Him a long night through.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And worship Him (a portion) of the night. And glorify Him through the livelong night.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: and worship Him for a watch of the night and glorify Him the night long.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: and in the night prostrate to Him; and exalt Him for a long night.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And during the night prostrate to Him and exalt Him a long [part of the] night.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Prostrate before Him and glorify Him extensively during the night.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And during part of the night adore Him, and give glory to Him (a) long (part of the) night.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: and during the night prostrate ourself before Him, and extol His glory for a long part of the night.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: And part of the night, prostrate thyself to Him; and glorify Him a long night through.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Wamina allayli faosjud lahu wasabbihhu laylan taweelan

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Surely men love what hastes away and forget the grievous day (ahead).

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Indeed these people love what they have, the fleeting one, and have forsaken the immensely important day behind them.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Surely these men love the hasty world, and leave be behind them a heavy day.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Verily those love the Herein, and leave in front of them a heavy day.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Verily! These (disbelievers) love the present life of this world, and put behind them a heavy Day (that will be hard).

27.author: Itani
27.text: As for these: they love the fleeting life, and leave behind a Heavy Day.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Verily they love (the good of this world) that is hastily obtainable and are oblivious of the burdensome Day ahead of them.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Verily, these love the present life of this world, and put behind them a heavy Day.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Lo! these love fleeting life, and put behind them (the remembrance of) a grievous day.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Indeed they love this transitory life, and disregard a heavy day that is ahead of them.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Indeed, these love the hasty life, and are heedless and leave behind them a heavy Day.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Indeed, these [disbelievers] love the immediate and leave behind them a grave Day.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: These people (disbelievers) love the worldly life and neglect the terrifying day which will come.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Surely these love the transitory and neglect a grievous day before them.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Those people [who are unmindful of God] aspire for immediate gains, and put behind them a Heavy Day.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: As to these, they love the fleeting life, and put away behind them a Day (that will be) hard.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Inna haolai yuhibboona alAAajilata wayatharoona waraahum yawman thaqeelan

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: We created them and fixed their bones and joints; and We could replace them when We like with others like them.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: It is We Who created them, and strengthened their skeleton; and We can replace them with others like them, whenever We will.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: We created them, and We strengthened their joints; and, when We will, We shall exchange their likes.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: It it We Who creared them and made them firm of make. And whenever We list, We can replace them with others like Unto them.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: It is We Who created them, and We have made them of strong built. And when We will, We can replace them with others like them with a complete replacement.

28.author: Itani
28.text: We created them, and strengthened their frame; and whenever We will, We can replace them with others like them.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: We created them and strengthened their joints; and whenever We wish, We can change their faces entirely.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: It is We Who created them, and We have made them of strong build. And when We will, We can replace them with others like them with a complete replacement.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: We, even We, created them, and strengthened their frame. And when We will, We can replace them, bringing others like them in their stead.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: We created them and strengthened their joints, and We will replace them with others like them whenever We like.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: We created them, and We strengthened their joints, but, when We will, We shall indeed exchange their likes.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: We have created them and strengthened their forms, and when We will, We can change their likenesses with [complete] alteration.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: We have created them and have given them strength. Had We wanted, We could have replaced them with another people like them.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: We created them and made firm their make, and when We please We will bring in their place the likes of them by a change.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: It was We who created them and made their constitution strong, but if We wish we can replace them with others like them.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: It is We Who created them, and We have made their joints strong; but, when We will, We can substitute the like of them by a complete change.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Nahnu khalaqnahum washadadna asrahum waitha shina baddalna amthalahum tabdeelan

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: This surely is a reminder: Therefore whosoever desires may take the way to his Lord.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: This is indeed an advice; so whoever wishes may take the path towards his Lord.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Surely this is a Reminder; so he who will, takes unto his Lord a way.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Verily this is an admonition; then whosoever Will, may choose a way Unto his Lord.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Verily! This (Verses of the Quran) is an admonition, so whosoever wills, let him take a Path to his Lord (Allah).

29.author: Itani
29.text: This is a reminder; so whoever wills, let him take a path to his Lord.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Verily this is an Exhortation; so let him who so will take a way to his Lord.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Verily, this is an admonition, so whosoever wills, let him take a path to his Lord.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: Lo! this is an Admonishment, that whosoever will may choose a way unto his Lord.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the way toward his Lord.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: This is indeed a Reminder, so that he who will, takes a path to his Lord.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: Indeed, this is a reminder, so he who wills may take to his Lord a way.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: This chapter is a reminder. Let those who want, seek guidance from their Lord.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: Surely this is a reminder, so whoever pleases takes to his Lord a way.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: This is a reminder. Let whoever wishes, take the right path to his Lord.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: This is an admonition: Whosoever will, let him take a (straight) Path to his Lord.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Inna hathihi tathkiratun faman shaa ittakhatha ila rabbihi sabeelan

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: But you will not desire except as God wills. Verily He is all-knowing and all-wise.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And what will you wish, except if Allah wills; indeed Allah is All Knowing, Wise.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: But you will not unless God wills; surely God is ever All-knowing, All-wise.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And ye will not, unless Allah willeth. Verily Allah is ever Knowing, Wise.'

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: But you cannot will, unless Allah wills. Verily, Allah is Ever All-Knowing, All-Wise.

30.author: Itani
30.text: Yet you cannot will, unless God wills. God is Knowing and Wise.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: But your willing shall be of no avail until Allah Himself so wills. Surely Allah is All-Knowing, Most Wise.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: But you cannot will, unless Allah wills. Verily, Allah is Ever All-Knowing, All-Wise.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Yet ye will not, unless Allah willeth. Lo! Allah is Knower, Wise.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: But you will not wish unless it is willed by Allah. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: Yet you will not unless Allah wills; surely, Allah is the Knower, the Wise.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And you do not will except that Allah wills. Indeed, Allah is ever Knowing and Wise.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: (The virtuous mentioned in this chapter) want only what God wants. God is All-knowing and All-wise.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And you do not please except that Allah please, surely Allah is Knowing, Wise;

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: But you cannot will it unless God wills [to show you that way] -- God is indeed all-knowing and wise --

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: But ye will not, except as Allah wills; for Allah is full of Knowledge and Wisdom.

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Wama tashaoona illa an yashaa Allahu inna Allaha kana AAaleeman hakeeman

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: He admits whosoever He will to His benevolence. But for the evil-doers He has prepared a painful punishment.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: He admits into His mercy, whomever He wills; and for the unjust He has kept prepared a painful punishment.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: For He admits into His mercy whomsoever He will; as for the evildoers, He has prepared for them a painful chastisement.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: He maketh whomsoever He listeth to enter His mercy. And the wrong-doers! for them He hath gotten ready a torment afflective.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: He will admit to His Mercy whom He will and as for the Zalimun, (polytheists, wrong-doers, etc.) He has prepared a painful torment.

31.author: Itani
31.text: He admits into His mercy whomever He wills. But as for the wrongdoers, He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: He admits to His Mercy whomsoever He pleases. As for the wrong-doers, He has prepared for them a grievous chastisement.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: He will admit to His mercy whom He wills and as for the wrongdoers -- He has prepared a painful torment.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: He maketh whom He will to enter His mercy, and for evil-doers hath prepared a painful doom.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: He admits whomever He wishes into His mercy, and He has prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: He is Merciful to whom He will; but, for the harmdoers He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: He admits whom He wills into His mercy; but the wrongdoers - He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: He admits to His mercy whomever He wants. For the unjust He has prepared a painful punishment.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: He makes whom He pleases to enter into His mercy; and (as for) the unjust, He has prepared for them a painful chastisement.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: He admits whoever He will into His grace and has prepared a painful punishment for the evil doers.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: He will admit to His Mercy whom He will; But the wrong-doers,- for them has He prepared a grievous Penalty.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Yudkhilu man yashao fee rahmatihi waalththalimeena aAAadda lahum AAathaban aleeman
